member.summary:
Defines whether table should be normalized to the amount of columns as defined in the table header. false by default
If true, this will insert empty cells in rows with fewer tables than the header row and remove cells that are exceeding the header column count. If false, this will use the row with the most columns to determine how many cells should be inserted in all other rows (default behavior).

member.name: P:Markdig.Extensions.Tables.PipeTableOptions.InferColumnWidthsFromSeparator
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether column widths should be inferred based on the number of dashes in the header separator row. Each column's width will be proportional to the dash count in its respective column.

member.summary: Attempts to match the parser at the current position.

member.name: M:Markdig.Extensions.Tables.PipeTableParser.PostProcess(Markdig.Parsers.InlineProcessor,Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.Inline,Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.Inline,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Performs the post process operation.

member.name: M:Markdig.Extensions.Tables.PipeTableParser.PromoteNestedLineBreaksToRootLevel(Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.ContainerInline)
member.summary: Promotes nested line breaks to root level.
member.remarks: Once any tracked table delimiter is nested, row boundaries may be nested in the same unresolved inline container. Promoting pipes without promoting those line breaks would let cell extraction walk across physical rows and produce incorrect cells.

member.name: M:Markdig.Extensions.Tables.PipeTableParser.PromoteNestedPipesToRootLevel(System.Collections.Generic.List{Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.Inline},Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.ContainerInline)
member.summary: Promotes nested pipe delimiters and tracked line breaks to root level.
member.remarks: Handles cases like `*a | b*|` and `**~$0.01** |` where a table boundary was emitted while an emphasis delimiter container was still open. After promotion, all tracked table delimiters become root-level siblings, so the table parser can count columns without depending on unresolved inline structure.

member.name: M:Markdig.Extensions.Tables.PipeTableParser.SplitContainerAtDelimiter(Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.Inline,Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.Inline)
member.summary: Splits a container at the delimiter, promoting the delimiter to root level.

member.name: M:Markdig.Extensions.Tables.PipeTableParser.CreateSplitContainer(Markdig.Syntax.Inlines.ContainerInline)
member.summary: Creates a plain container to wrap content split from the source container.

member.name: T:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState
member.summary: Defines the result of parsing a line for a .

member.name: F:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState.None
member.summary: The parser does not accept the line for this block. No line content is consumed by this result.

member.name: F:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState.Skip
member.summary: Skips this parser for the current line and continues with the next parser/candidate block.

member.name: F:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState.Continue
member.summary: The parser accepts the line and keeps the block open. For leaf blocks, the current line is appended to the block.

member.name: F:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState.ContinueDiscard
member.summary: The parser accepts the line and keeps the block open, but the line is consumed and not appended.

member.name: F:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState.Break
member.summary: The parser ends the block and keeps the current line available for further parsing.

member.name: F:Markdig.Parsers.BlockState.BreakDiscard
member.summary: The parser ends the block and consumes the current line.

member.name: M:Markdig.Syntax.ContainerBlock.UpdateSpanFromChildren(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Updates this container span from its child block spans.
member.param: When true, updates descendant container blocks and inline containers recursively before updating this container.
When true, preserves this container current span and only expands it to include children. When false, recomputes from children only.
member.returns: true when this container span changed; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf16(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Text.Rune@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Decodes the at the beginning of the provided UTF-16 source buffer.

returns.para: If the source buffer begins with a valid UTF-16 encoded scalar value, returns , and outs via the decoded and via the number of s used in the input buffer to encode the .
If the source buffer is empty or contains only a standalone UTF-16 high surrogate character, returns , and outs via and via the length of the input buffer.
If the source buffer begins with an ill-formed UTF-16 encoded scalar value, returns , and outs via and via the number of s used in the input buffer to encode the ill-formed sequence.

member.remarks: The general calling convention is to call this method in a loop, slicing the buffer by elements on each iteration of the loop. On each iteration of the loop will contain the real scalar value if successfully decoded, or it will contain if the data could not be successfully decoded. This pattern provides convenient automatic U+FFFD substitution of invalid sequences while iterating through the loop.

member.name: M:System.Text.Rune.DecodeFromUtf8(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Text.Rune@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Decodes the at the beginning of the provided UTF-8 source buffer.

returns.para: If the source buffer begins with a valid UTF-8 encoded scalar value, returns , and outs via the decoded and via the number of s used in the input buffer to encode the .
If the source buffer is empty or contains only a partial UTF-8 subsequence, returns , and outs via and via the length of the input buffer.
If the source buffer begins with an ill-formed UTF-8 encoded scalar value, returns , and outs via and via the number of s used in the input buffer to encode the ill-formed sequence.

member.remarks: The general calling convention is to call this method in a loop, slicing the buffer by elements on each iteration of the loop. On each iteration of the loop will contain the real scalar value if successfully decoded, or it will contain if the data could not be successfully decoded. This pattern provides convenient automatic U+FFFD substitution of invalid sequences while iterating through the loop.

member.name: M:System.Text.Rune.DecodeLastFromUtf16(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Text.Rune@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Decodes the at the end of the provided UTF-16 source buffer.
member.remarks: This method is very similar to , but it allows the caller to loop backward instead of forward. The typical calling convention is that on each iteration of the loop, the caller should slice off the final elements of the buffer.

member.name: M:System.Text.Rune.DecodeLastFromUtf8(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Text.Rune@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Decodes the at the end of the provided UTF-8 source buffer.
member.remarks: This method is very similar to , but it allows the caller to loop backward instead of forward. The typical calling convention is that on each iteration of the loop, the caller should slice off the final elements of the buffer.
